change_summary: detail_evidence expanded from 1183 to 1618 chars (+37%). Decider applied c3 review recommendations (commit 0629e67, 2026-04-17 17:41): (1) Domain number corrected #25 → 16; (2) quantitative parasitism argument added — f=c/(2D) reproduces 11.787 Hz only when D=12,717 km, matching Earth diameter but not any dome parameter. This c4 verifies the patch is correct and probes the strengthened text for new attack surface.
our_argument_summary: Our review says the 11-12 Hz region is an inter-harmonic Schumann minimum (standard spherical-cavity physics), that the first harmonic at 14.3 Hz directly falsifies any cutoff at 11.7 Hz, and that the dome monitor hardcodes both predicted and observed values. New in c4: the detail_evidence now explicitly shows that f = c/(2D) reproduces 11.787 Hz only when D = 12,717 km, which is essentially Earth's diameter — none of the dome's own parameters (firmament height 9,086 km, disc radius 20,015 km, 2× firmament 18,172 km) produce this frequency. The parasitism is now unambiguous.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Domain 16 in the live-power dump lists predicted 11.788 Hz and observed 11.7869 Hz with tolerance 1.0% and status PASS. No MT data is fetched from any external source — both sides are baked in. Source attribution is Geometrics MT data (raw-text/02-wins.txt line 75) but there is no corresponding API call.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded. The specific numbers 11.788 and 11.7869 are present in the live-power output, differing by only 0.093% — within the 1.0% tolerance by construction.
data_sources_used: "Geometrics MT data" as stated attribution; no actual fetch
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: 1.0% tolerance listed but irrelevant with near-identical pred/obs values.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Our text says 'hardcodes both predicted and observed values as 11.7 Hz with 0.0% error'. The raw-text actually shows 11.788 predicted vs 11.7869 observed — a 0.093% error that rounds to 0.0%. This is minor — the rounded statement is technically correct — but a nitpicky defender could note the exact values are not identical.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: raw-text/08-live-power.txt Domain 16 shows Predicted: 11.788 Hz | Observed: 11.7869 Hz | Status: PASS with no data-fetch URL. No IRIS, Geometrics-API, or MT dataset call is present.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Inter-harmonic spectral minimum between the 7.83 Hz Schumann fundamental and the 14.3 Hz first harmonic is standard spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity physics (Schumann 1952; Balser & Wagner 1960; Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002). The dome relabels this feature as a 'disc resonance ceiling' without altering any numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Magnetotelluric spectral features in the 10-20 Hz band have been published since the 1960s and are indexed in the Chave & Jones (2012) reference text. The dome adopts the known inter-harmonic minimum as a 'prediction.' monitor.py bakes the target frequency on the same commit as the observation line, so temporal priority cannot be established from git history.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: f = c/(2D) yields 11.787 Hz only when D ≈ 12,717 km. Verified by direct calculation: c/(2·12717 km) = 11.7871 Hz. None of the dome's own documented parameters produce this: firmament_height 9,086 km → 16.50 Hz; disc_radius 20,015 km → 7.49 Hz; 2·firmament_height 18,172 km → 8.25 Hz. The value 12,717 km is essentially Earth's diameter (closer to polar 12,713.6 km than to mean 12,742 km). The current detail_evidence now states this parasitism explicitly (good), though it labels 12,717 km as 'Earth's mean diameter' when polar diameter is actually the better match (see minor hole).

holes_found.description: IMPRECISE EARTH-DIAMETER REFERENCE: The new parasitism sentence reads 'D = 12,717 km — essentially Earth's mean diameter (12,742 km, a 0.2% residual).' Actually, 12,717 km is a much closer match to Earth's POLAR diameter (12,713.6 km, residual 0.027%) than to its mean (volumetric) diameter (12,742 km, residual 0.196%). A reader who looks up these numbers will find our match-to-polar argument tighter than our match-to-mean. A sharper reader would also notice that Earth's polar diameter, not mean, is the smaller value the dome's back-derivation actually lands on, which makes the parasitism claim even stronger — the dome silently uses a specific Earth dimension (polar), not just 'roughly Earth-sized.'
holes_found.recommendation: Two options. (a) Change 'Earth's mean diameter (12,742 km, a 0.2% residual)' to 'Earth's diameter (polar diameter 12,713.6 km, 0.03% residual; mean diameter 12,742 km, 0.2% residual).' This is the quantitatively-strongest version. (b) Simpler: 'essentially Earth's diameter (within 0.2% of the mean value, within 0.03% of the polar value).' WIN-001's finding already uses the unqualified 'Earth's diameter' phrasing — harmonizing WIN-008 to match would also improve cross-WIN consistency.

holes_found.description: HARDCODE ROUNDING: Our detail_evidence says 'hardcodes both predicted and observed values as 11.7 Hz with 0.0% error.' The raw-text Domain 16 output actually shows predicted=11.788 Hz, observed=11.7869 Hz. These are NOT identical — they differ by 0.011 Hz (0.093%, rounds to 0.0%). A literate dome defender could claim 'your review misrepresents the numbers — they're 11.788 and 11.7869, not both 11.7 with 0.0% error.' The functional point (both values baked in, no real measurement) is intact, but the specific framing is slightly inaccurate.
holes_found.recommendation: Change 'hardcodes both predicted and observed values as 11.7 Hz with 0.0% error' to 'hardcodes predicted = 11.788 Hz, observed = 11.7869 Hz (0.09% error, rounds to 0.0%) — both values baked into the script rather than measured.' This is more accurate and actually strengthens the hardcoded-by-construction point by showing the numbers are too precise to be a real measurement.

holes_found.description: TLDR IDENTICAL-VALUES LANGUAGE: tldr_evidence says 'The dome's code hardcodes both predicted and observed values as identical.' Strictly, 11.788 and 11.7869 are not identical — they differ by 0.093%. The more-accurate TLDR would say 'nearly identical' or 'effectively identical.' Flagged minor because the functional point (no real measurement) is preserved and TLDRs are allowed to simplify.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider 'The dome's code hardcodes both predicted and observed values to within 0.1% — a match too tight to be a real measurement.' Optional.

holes_found.description: CARRY-FORWARD FROM C3: generic post-hoc boilerplate dataset list still present. The parenthetical '(IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.)' inside detail_verdict_text is copy-pasted across 51+ WINs; for WIN-008 only 'telluric records' is relevant. Not incorrect, just imprecise. The decider has not yet addressed this in the c3 patch batch, so it carries to c4.
holes_found.recommendation: As noted in c3: tighten per-WIN to 'a field-specific published dataset (MT/ELF spectral literature)' or move the grab-bag into Part 3.1b and cite it. Low priority.

stronger_arguments.description: PROPAGATE PARASITISM TO WIN-009: WIN-001 (Tesla) and WIN-008 (telluric cutoff) now both explicitly flag the Earth-diameter parasitism. WIN-009 (telluric ~12 Hz peak) shares the same 11.78 Hz feature but its detail_evidence does not currently state the parasitism. Adding a one-sentence parasitism note to WIN-009 would unify the three-WIN triplet and ensure a reader browsing any one of them encounters the same quantitative rebuttal.
sources: WIN-001, WIN-008 current detail_evidence in data/wins.json
WIN-009 detail_evidence lacks the parasitism sentence

stronger_arguments.description: EMPHASIZE THE DOME'S OWN ADMISSION OF REMOVING CIRCULAR DOUBLE-COUNTING: raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 36 records 'Voluntarily removed circular reasoning (Tesla f->T->f) and double-counted wins.' The dome author has admitted in release notes that WIN-001 was implicated in circular reasoning. WIN-008 and WIN-009 still ride on the same f=c/(2D)=11.787 Hz feature with Earth-diameter D. The dome's own admission is a rhetorical gift: we can cite the author's own statement against the surviving double-count. This is a potential detail_extra addition for the WIN-008/009 group or for the kill-shot section.

kernel_of_truth.description: The 10-12 Hz band genuinely shows reduced spectral power density relative to the adjacent 7.83 Hz and 14.3 Hz Schumann peaks. The dome correctly identifies a real feature of the natural ELF spectrum.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Standard spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity physics fully predicts this inter-harmonic minimum. The dome's only formula for the feature (f = c/2D) reproduces 11.787 Hz only when D ≈ 12,717 km — a value that is not one of its own documented parameters (disc radius 20,015, firmament height 9,086, 2·firmament 18,172). The closest match for D is Earth's polar diameter (12,713.6 km, 0.03% residual). The 'derivation' is a relabel of spherical-Earth geometry. Additionally, the label 'sharp cutoff' is directly falsified by the robust first Schumann harmonic at 14.3 Hz.

deeper_analysis:
C4 re-review triggered by decider commit 0629e67 (2026-04-17 17:41) which applied c3 recommendations: Domain number fix (#25 → 16) and quantitative parasitism sentence (D=12,717 km, not any dome parameter). Both fixes are correct and substantively strengthen the argument. The verdict (Refuted by Data) still holds unambiguously on scientific grounds (14.3 Hz first Schumann harmonic directly falsifies a cutoff at 11.7 Hz) and on post-hoc grounds (indexed public MT spectral data, hardcoded predicted/observed values on the same commit as the fetch line).

Quantitative verification: re-ran all four key calculations with c = 299,792,458 m/s. c/(2·12717 km) = 11.7871 Hz ✓. c/(2·9086 km) = 16.4975 Hz ≈ 16.50 ✓. c/(2·20015 km) = 7.4892 Hz ≈ 7.49 ✓. c/(2·18172 km) = 8.2487 Hz ≈ 8.25 ✓. Reverse-derivation for f = 11.787 Hz gives D = 12,717.08 km, matching the text.

One subtle quantitative finding: 12,717 km is NOT Earth's mean diameter (12,742 km; residual 0.2%) but rather much closer to Earth's POLAR diameter (12,713.6 km; residual 0.027%). The current text labels it mean diameter, which is 0.2% off. Polar diameter is 10× closer. Flagged as minor hole with concrete fix options.

The c3-rated defense (advocate_mode defense_survives=3) has been largely neutralized by the parasitism sentence. A dome defender who accepts our methodology principles can no longer plausibly claim the dome's derivation is independent of spherical-Earth geometry — the derivation requires Earth's diameter as an input the dome does not document. Current defense rating ~2.

Unresolved c3 carry-forward: the generic post-hoc boilerplate dataset list is still present. Not blocking. Remains minor.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender, conceding our methodology principles, can now only argue: (1) The 12,717 km 'disc thickness' is a derivable dome parameter — specifically, it equals twice the projection of firmament curvature onto the horizontal at some radial distance we haven't computed, so the apparent Earth-diameter match is coincidental. This is extremely weak because the dome has never published such a derivation — it is purely speculative. (2) The first Schumann harmonic at 14.3 Hz is an ionospheric feature, not a disc-cavity feature; the dome model does not deny ionospheric EM activity, so 14.3 Hz doesn't falsify the dome's 11.7 Hz cutoff for the disc-cavity mode specifically. This requires the dome to have a two-cavity model where the disc and ionosphere are independent resonators — which the dome has also not published. Rating: the defense now requires the dome to introduce new mechanisms that don't exist in the current publication, so both attacks fail on our site's actual text. The defense rating is down from c3's 3 to ~2.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Minor asymmetry: WIN-001 finding says 'Earth's diameter, not any dome parameter' (unqualified); WIN-008 detail_evidence says 'Earth's mean diameter (12,742 km, a 0.2% residual)' (qualified, slightly less accurate — polar is closer). WIN-009 detail_evidence cites Chave & Jones 2012 and flags the inter-harmonic-minimum argument but does not yet include the parasitism sentence. Unifying the three-WIN language would strengthen all three (see stronger_arguments item 1). No contradictions — only missed opportunity for consistency.

claims_checked: f = c/(2D) with D = 12,717 km yields 11.787 Hz (our text's key parasitism claim)
f = c/(2D) with D = 9,086 km (firmament_height) yields 16.50 Hz
f = c/(2D) with D = 20,015 km (disc_radius) yields 7.49 Hz
f = c/(2D) with D = 18,172 km (2 × firmament) yields 8.25 Hz
Earth's mean diameter is 12,742 km; residual vs 12,717 is 0.196% ≈ 0.2%
Earth's polar diameter (12,713.6 km) vs 12,717: residual = 0.027% (tighter than mean)
Reverse: f = 11.787 Hz requires D = 12,717.08 km — exact reproduction of our text's figure
quantitative_verification.computations: Used c = 299,792,458 m/s. All forward and reverse calculations verified to 4+ significant figures. The key new finding is that the 12,717 km value matches Earth's POLAR diameter much more tightly than mean diameter (0.03% vs 0.2%). Our current text labels the match as 'mean diameter' which is the less-precise option. This does not change the argument's structure — it just means we could be more precise.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Labeling 12,717 km as 'essentially Earth's mean diameter' is imprecise; polar diameter is the tighter match. See minor hole #1 for recommendation.

citations_verified: Chave & Jones (2012) The Magnetotelluric Method: DOI 10.1017/CBO9781139020138 is a valid Cambridge Books Online identifier; the book exists and is the standard MT reference
dome author's definition URL https://john09289.github.io/predictions is the dome site itself (in-text reference, no external validation needed)
Internal anchor href='#posthoc-standard' points to Part 3.1b where the post-hoc standard is defined — present in sections.json
